task:
Build out Pulse Property roadmap — 17 deliverables across 3 stages (single iterate, --stages=3 --max=3).

STAGE 1 — Backend foundation + Subscription system (binayah-api + binayah-properties)
1. New MarketReportSubscription Mongoose model on binayah-api: email, name, phone (opt), intents[] (buy/rent/sell/invest), areas[], propertyTypes[], budgetMin, budgetMax, confirmedAt, unsubscribedAt, lastSentAt, unsubToken (random), createdAt
2. Endpoints on binayah-api:
   - POST /api/market-report/subscribe — store sub, fire confirmation email (double opt-in)
   - GET  /api/market-report/confirm/:token — set confirmedAt
   - GET  /api/market-report/unsubscribe/:token — set unsubscribedAt
   - POST /api/admin/market-report/send-weekly — admin trigger to send digest now (also wired to a Render cron job at Mon 09:00 UTC)
3. Email service helper (binayah-api): uses existing SMTP_* env vars; sends confirmation + weekly digest with personalized content (top movers in user's areas, new launches matching property/budget, 1 featured guide). Plain HTML, unsub link in footer.
4. Yield-by-area aggregation endpoint on binayah-api: GET /api/dld/areas/:slug/yield — computes avg rent / avg sale per area; flag samples <50 with `lowConfidence:true`.
5. OG image route on binayah-properties: /api/og/pulse — Next.js route returning a PNG card with Pulse headline metric, used for share unfurls.
6. PDF render route on binayah-properties: /api/pdf/pulse — server-renders a Pulse summary PDF (uses puppeteer-core or pdfkit; pick whichever is lighter).
7. WeeklySubscribeForm component on binayah-properties: 1-step form with name+email required + optional phone + intent chips + areas multiselect (autocomplete from /api/communities) + property type chips + budget range; replaces every existing "Get a free market report" form (currently on /pulse, /pulse/trending, /pulse/emirate, etc.). Confirmation-pending screen after submit.
8. /account/market-reports page (logged-in users) to manage their subscription preferences.

STAGE 2 — Pulse Overview enrichment + Emirate Dubai detail page
9. Sub-nav with Emirate tabs on every Pulse page: Dubai active, AUH/SHJ/RAK/Ajman with "Coming soon" badges + disabled links.
10. Sentiment indicator (Bullish / Neutral / Bearish) — derive from 3-month MoM avgPpsf trend; appears on Overview top + Emirate page.
11. Most-active-community KPI on Overview — read DldArea sorted by totalSales desc, take top.
12. Top-communities leaderboard on Overview + Emirate (price/sqft, yield, YoY, volume) — uses existing /api/dld/areas with new yield endpoint from stage 1.
13. Quick ticker on Overview (top 3 movers, horizontal scroll, links to communities) — reuses Trending movers logic.
14. Off-plan share KPI on Overview + Emirate — compute % of Project where status is off-plan.
15. /pulse/emirate/dubai page — sentiment, KPI strip (transactions YTD, avg ppsf, avg yield, YoY growth, off-plan share, communities tracked), 24-mo price trend chart, monthly transaction volume chart, community highlights (highest price / best yield / fastest growth / best value), developer highlights (most active / fastest growing / most off-plan / best yield), developer comparison table, "why invest in Dubai" section, community leaderboard, featured community cards, lead form using new WeeklySubscribeForm.
16. Yield-by-community leaderboard on Compare + Emirate — uses stage 1 endpoint; shows lowConfidence flag for thin samples.
17. Featured insight panel + share buttons on Trending + Overview — pulls latest News article; share buttons (WhatsApp, X, LinkedIn, Copy Link) wire OG image; UTM tags on every share URL.

STAGE 3 — Calculator overhaul + Compare presets + Polish
18. Calculator benchmarks — replace hardcoded S&P/Savings with three labeled lines: "S&P 500 5-yr avg" (10.2%), "UAE Savings 5-yr avg" (3.5%), "Dubai property 5-yr avg" (real, derived from DLD MoM trend); each gets a tooltip with source.
19. Calculator verdict — replace single "Strong Investment" badge with explainer block: "Rental yield: X% (vs Dubai avg Y%)", "5-yr appreciation forecast: Z%", "vs S&P 500 5-yr: ±W%". Verdict pill (Strong/Moderate/Weak) driven by explicit scoring rule documented in code.
20. Calculator share — Share button copies URL with calc state encoded in querystring; recipient lands on filled-in calc. WhatsApp share includes 3-line preview text.
21. Calculator email — POST /api/calculator/email on binayah-properties → server-side HTML email with full calc result; lead logged to MarketReportSubscription as a "calculator-lead" intent.
22. Calculator projection lines — add 3-yr and 5-yr projection lines on the results chart, linear extrapolation from DLD trend with disclaimer.
23. Compare presets — 4 curated arrays: "Waterfront Luxury" (Palm Jumeirah, Dubai Marina, Bluewaters Island), "High Yield" (JVC, Dubai Sports City, Dubai South), "Urban Core" (Downtown Dubai, Business Bay, DIFC), "Emerging Stars" (Dubai Hills Estate, Tilal Al Ghaf, Town Square). Click loads them into the comparison.
24. New project launches list on Trending — Project.find({}).sort({launchDate: -1}).limit(6) with developer, area, starting price, payment plan.
25. Share buttons cleanup site-wide — replace "Embed" with "Save as PDF" (calls /api/pdf/pulse from stage 1); add UTM tagging on every share URL across Pulse pages.
26. Guide detail layout polish — heading hierarchy, embedded stats blocks pulling from DLD API by mentioned area, related-areas footer with placeKind links.

CONSTRAINTS
- No multi-emirate data exists yet — only Dubai pipelines work. Other emirate tabs MUST be visible-but-disabled with "Coming soon" badges. Don't fake numbers.
- All new UI strings must be in en/ar/zh/ru locale files (eslint i18next/no-literal-string blocks Vercel build otherwise).
- New env vars (none expected — SMTP_* + AWS_* + MONGODB_URI already on Render) — flag if any new ones are needed.
- Render cron for weekly send: register in render.yaml as a cronJob service (existing pattern: dld-refresh runs daily 03:00 UTC).
- Email service uses existing SMTP_HOST/PORT/USER/PASS already configured for inquiries.
- Existing "Get a free market report" form must be REPLACED, not duplicated, so we don't end up with two competing forms on /pulse.
- All routes must be locale-aware (use @/navigation, not next/link directly).
- The /pulse/emirate/dubai page should be the marquee piece — match the design quality bar from .claude/DESIGN_PRINCIPLES.md if it exists.

stages.name: Backend foundation + subscription system
stages.spec:
STAGE 1 — Backend foundation + subscription system. Cross-repo: binayah-api (Fastify+Mongoose, push to its main) AND binayah-properties (Next.js, push to main). DEV must commit each repo separately.

## Why this will be good
The weekly digest is the LEAD-GEN engine for everything downstream — every Pulse page in stage 2 will end with this form. So this stage must (a) actually deliver an email people want to read, (b) feel as confidence-inspiring as a Linear or Stripe onboarding, (c) make double-opt-in feel like a feature ("check your inbox — we just sent a teaser of next Monday's report"), not a bureaucratic step. The Confirmation-pending screen and the unsubscribe page are first impressions; treat them like first impressions. Production-ready means: empty/error/loading/auth states all designed; unsub works in one click with no login; the form survives a 500 from the API with a clear retry; mobile thumb-reachable.

## What DEV must build

### A. binayah-api repo (`/Users/zoop/binayah-api`)

1. **Mongoose model** `src/models/MarketReportSubscription.ts`:
   - Fields: `email` (lowercased, indexed unique on `(email, unsubscribedAt: null)`), `name`, `phone?`, `intents: ('buy'|'rent'|'sell'|'invest'|'calculator-lead')[]`, `areas: string[]` (community slugs), `propertyTypes: string[]` (e.g. Apartment, Villa, Townhouse), `budgetMin?: number`, `budgetMax?: number`, `confirmToken: string` (random 32-byte hex, indexed), `unsubToken: string` (separate random token, indexed), `confirmedAt?: Date`, `unsubscribedAt?: Date`, `lastSentAt?: Date`, `createdAt: Date`, `source: string` (e.g. "pulse-overview", "pulse-emirate-dubai", "calculator")
   - Generate tokens with `crypto.randomBytes(32).toString('hex')`.

2. **Routes** `src/routes/market-report.ts` (register in `src/index.ts`):
   - `POST /api/market-report/subscribe` — body `{ email, name, phone?, intents, areas, propertyTypes, budgetMin?, budgetMax?, source? }`. If existing record for this email is unconfirmed, regenerate confirmToken and re-send. If confirmed and unsubscribed, reactivate (set unsubscribedAt=null, regenerate confirmToken, require re-confirm). Return `{ ok: true, status: 'pending' | 'already-confirmed' }`. Validate email shape; return 400 with helpful message on bad input.
   - `GET /api/market-report/confirm/:token` — set confirmedAt, return JSON `{ ok: true }` (binayah-properties shows the success page).
   - `GET /api/market-report/unsubscribe/:token` — set unsubscribedAt, return JSON `{ ok: true }`.
   - `GET /api/admin/market-report/subscribers` (preHandler: requireAdmin via `x-admin-secret`) — list with filters.
   - `POST /api/admin/market-report/send-weekly` (preHandler: requireAdmin) — iterate confirmed+!unsubscribed subscribers, build personalized digest payload (top movers in their `areas`, new launches matching their `propertyTypes` + budget, 1 featured guide), call email helper, update lastSentAt, return `{ sent: N, failed: M }`.

3. **Email helper** `src/services/emailService.ts`:
   - Use existing `SMTP_HOST/PORT/USER/PASS` env vars (already on Render). If no `SMTP_HOST` is set, fall back to `nodemailer.createTransport({ service: 'gmail', auth: { user: SMTP_USER, pass: SMTP_PASS } })` to match the existing inquiry-email pattern.
   - Two HTML templates as functions returning `{ subject, html, text }`:
     - `confirmationEmail({ name, confirmUrl, unsubUrl })` — short, friendly, single CTA button (gold gradient, brand styling) "Confirm your subscription". Mention what they'll get on Monday.
     - `weeklyDigestEmail({ name, topMovers, newLaunches, featuredGuide, unsubUrl })` — header with brand mark, sections with numbers (e.g. "Dubai Marina · AED 1,580/sqft · +2.4% MoM"), CTA back to `/pulse/emirate/dubai?utm_source=email&utm_medium=digest&utm_campaign=weekly`, plain unsub link in footer.
   - All links must point at `FRONTEND_URL` env var (default `https://staging.binayahhub.com`) — `${FRONTEND_URL}/pulse/confirm/<token>` and `${FRONTEND_URL}/pulse/unsubscribe/<token>`.

4. **Yield-by-area aggregation** `src/routes/dld.ts` — add `GET /api/dld/areas/:slug/yield`:
   - Compute avg sale price/sqft and avg annual rent/sqft from `DldTransaction` collection where `community === slug` over the last 12 months. yield = avgRentPsf / avgSalePsf.
   - Include `salesSampleSize`, `rentSampleSize`. If either < 50, set `lowConfidence: true`.
   - Cache for 1 hour in-memory (Map with TTL) — DLD numbers don't change intra-day. If a cache layer already exists in dld.ts, follow that pattern.

5. **Render cron** — append to `/Users/zoop/binayah-api/render.yaml`:
   ```yaml
   - type: cron
     name: weekly-market-report
     runtime: node
     schedule: "0 9 * * 1"  # Mon 09:00 UTC
     buildCommand: npm install
     startCommand: 'curl -X POST -H "x-admin-secret: $ADMIN_SECRET" https://binayah-api.onrender.com/api/admin/market-report/send-weekly'
     envVars:
       - key: ADMIN_SECRET
         sync: false
   ```
   (Match existing cron pattern in the file if there's already one for dld-refresh; if not, this is the template.)

### B. binayah-properties repo (`/Users/zoop/binayah-properties`)

6. **`WeeklySubscribeForm` component** at `src/components/WeeklySubscribeForm.tsx`:
   - Single client component. Props: `{ source: string; defaultAreas?: string[]; defaultPropertyTypes?: string[]; variant?: 'inline' | 'card' }`.
   - One-step form. Fields: name (required), email (required), phone (optional, with country code), intent chips (multi-select: Buy / Rent / Sell / Invest), areas autocomplete (`/api/communities` from Fastify, multi-select, shows slug+name), property type chips (Apartment / Villa / Townhouse / Penthouse), budget dual-handle range slider (AED 0 – AED 20M, optional, with "any budget" pseudo-state).
   - Submit POSTs `${NEXT_PUBLIC_API_URL}/api/market-report/subscribe`. Loading spinner inline during request. On success replace form with `ConfirmationPending` panel: large checkmark, headline "Check your inbox", body "We sent a confirmation link to <email>. Click it within 24 hours to start receiving Monday's report.", small "Didn't get it? Resend" link (POSTs again with same payload).
   - Error: if API returns non-200, render inline red-bordered banner above the submit button with the API's message; never silent. If network fails, generic "We couldn't reach our servers. Try again in a moment."
   - Empty/initial state copy is specific: "Get Monday's Dubai market read in your inbox." Not "Subscribe now."
   - Apply DESIGN_SYSTEM.md: brand-green primary submit button gradient `linear-gradient(135deg, #0B3D2E, #1A7A5A)`, gold accent for the eyebrow label. Lift on hover. 44px tap targets. Areas autocomplete must work on mobile (no hover-only patterns).
   - i18n: every visible string under a new `weeklyReport` key in `messages/en.json` (and ar/zh/ru — translate via the same approach the codebase already uses; if existing `useTranslations` pattern wraps strings, use it). Bilingual: even the chip labels.
   - Variant `inline` is for embedding inside another section; variant `card` wraps the form in a `bg-card border border-border/50 rounded-2xl shadow-xl p-8` container with the eyebrow + heading + form.

7. **Confirmation + unsubscribe pages** (locale-aware, under `[locale]/pulse/`):
   - `src/app/[locale]/pulse/confirm/[token]/page.tsx` — server component. Hits `${API_BASE_URL}/api/market-report/confirm/:token`. Renders success state ("You're on the list. First report Monday morning.") or expired/invalid state with a CTA to resubscribe. Both states are designed surfaces, not raw text.
   - `src/app/[locale]/pulse/unsubscribe/[token]/page.tsx` — server component. Calls `${API_BASE_URL}/api/market-report/unsubscribe/:token`. Renders confirmation ("You've been removed. We're sorry to see you go.") with a single button to resubscribe, and an optional "why are you leaving?" textarea (POSTs to a new `/api/market-report/feedback` — OK to defer this textarea endpoint to stage 3 if tight; design it but make optional).

8. **OG image route** `src/app/api/og/pulse/route.ts`:
   - Use `@vercel/og` (`ImageResponse` from `next/og` — already supported in Next 15). NO puppeteer.
   - Returns 1200×630 PNG with: brand-green gradient bg, brand mark, headline metric (e.g. "Dubai property +2.4% this month"), subtitle, small Pulse wordmark.
   - Accepts `?metric=...&value=...&trend=...` query params; sane defaults if missing.
   - Cache header: `public, max-age=3600, s-maxage=3600`.

9. **PDF route** `src/app/api/pdf/pulse/route.ts`:
   - Use `pdfkit` (lightweight, no headless Chrome). NO puppeteer.
   - Returns a 2-3 page summary PDF: header (brand), Dubai KPI strip (transactions YTD, avg ppsf, yield, YoY, off-plan share — fetch live from `/api/dld/...`), top 5 communities table, footer with date and binayahhub.com URL.
   - Filename: `Content-Disposition: attachment; filename="binayah-pulse-<yyyy-mm-dd>.pdf"`.
   - This route is consumed by share buttons in stage 3; in stage 1 we just need the route working so QA can curl it.

10. **`/account/market-reports` page** `src/app/[locale]/account/market-reports/page.tsx` + client:
    - Auth-required (NextAuth `getServerSession`; redirect to `/signin?callbackUrl=` if missing). 
    - Server-fetches the user's subscription by their email (proxy to a new tiny `GET /api/market-report/by-email` on Fastify, admin-secret guarded — DEV decides whether to proxy via a Next API route to avoid leaking the secret; preferred pattern: Next.js API route `/api/account/market-reports` reads session, calls Fastify with admin-secret server-side).
    - Page lets the user edit areas/propertyTypes/intents/budget via the same `WeeklySubscribeForm` component in an "edit mode" variant, plus a clearly-styled red-bordered "Unsubscribe" button (modal confirmation per DESIGN_SYSTEM.md).
    - Empty state if no subscription yet: warm copy + embedded inline `WeeklySubscribeForm` so they can sign up right there.

## Design intent (apply DESIGN_PRINCIPLES + DESIGN_SYSTEM)
- "Show the data, not a symbol of it": confirmation page can show a tiny preview of next Monday's report (e.g., 3 stat tiles).
- "Empty states are features": /account/market-reports empty state is not "You have no subscription" — it's an inline subscription form with a 1-line value prop.
- "Every pixel intentional": don't ship default Tailwind form inputs — match `SignInClient.tsx` input style (border, focus ring, rounded-xl).
- "Mobile is the default": chip groups must wrap; budget slider must be thumb-friendly; autocomplete dropdown must not be cut off by viewport.
- "The copy is the UI": every label is specific. No "Submit" — "Send me Monday's report". No "Required" — "We need this". No "No items" anywhere.

## Out of scope (stage 1)
- Wiring the form into `/pulse`, `/pulse/trending`, `/pulse/calculator`, etc. — that's stage 2/3. Stage 1 only ships the component and the pages that USE it directly (`/account/market-reports`, confirm/unsubscribe).
- Render cron actually firing on schedule — we register it in render.yaml. Verifying scheduled execution is post-ship.
- Stage 2's emirate page consuming the form (also stage 2).

## demoPath (QA verification)
1. POST `http://localhost:3001/api/market-report/subscribe` with `{ email: "qa@test.binayah.com", name: "QA", intents: ["invest"], areas: ["dubai-marina"], propertyTypes: ["Apartment"], source: "qa-test" }` → expect 200 with `{ ok: true, status: "pending" }`. Verify a row exists in MongoDB `marketreportsubscriptions` collection with confirmToken + unsubToken set, confirmedAt null. (Note: Atlas DNS unreachable in sandbox — mark `severity: "env"` if it 500s and check logic via code review only.)
2. GET `/api/market-report/confirm/<token>` → 200; row has confirmedAt set.
3. Visit `http://localhost:3000/account/market-reports` after `/api/test/auth-seed` → see the form (or current subscription if seeded).
4. Visit `http://localhost:3000/api/og/pulse?metric=Dubai+property&value=%2B2.4%25&trend=up` → returns a PNG image, 1200×630.
5. Visit `http://localhost:3000/api/pdf/pulse` → downloads PDF, opens, shows KPI strip with real numbers.
6. Visit `http://localhost:3000/pulse/confirm/<token>` and `/pulse/unsubscribe/<token>` → designed pages, not raw JSON.
7. Mobile 390px on `/account/market-reports` form → no horizontal scroll, all chips reachable.

## filesTouched (expected; DEV may add more if needed)
**binayah-api:**
- `src/models/MarketReportSubscription.ts` (new)
- `src/routes/market-report.ts` (new)
- `src/routes/dld.ts` (edit — add yield route)
- `src/services/emailService.ts` (new or extend existing)
- `src/index.ts` (register new routes)
- `render.yaml` (add cron)

**binayah-properties:**
- `src/components/WeeklySubscribeForm.tsx` (new)
- `src/components/WeeklySubscribeFormFields.tsx` if split (DEV decides)
- `src/app/[locale]/pulse/confirm/[token]/page.tsx` (new)
- `src/app/[locale]/pulse/unsubscribe/[token]/page.tsx` (new)
- `src/app/[locale]/account/market-reports/page.tsx` + client (new)
- `src/app/api/og/pulse/route.ts` (new)
- `src/app/api/pdf/pulse/route.ts` (new)
- `src/app/api/account/market-reports/route.ts` (new — session-gated proxy)
- `messages/en.json`, `messages/ar.json`, `messages/zh.json`, `messages/ru.json` (extend with `weeklyReport` namespace)
- `package.json` (add `@vercel/og` if not present, `pdfkit` + `@types/pdfkit`)

## Constraints reminder
- `@vercel/og` not puppeteer; `pdfkit` not puppeteer.
- Use `@/navigation` for all internal navigation.
- Wrap all visible strings in `useTranslations` / `t()` — eslint i18next/no-literal-string blocks Vercel build.
- Atlas DNS unreachable in local sandbox — Mongo-touching APIs may 500 in QA; mark env.
- Diff cap ~1500 lines for this stage.
deferredItems: PDF route — moved to Stage 3 with @react-pdf/renderer or alternative; pdfkit incompatible with Next.js 15 Turbopack

stages.name: Pulse Overview enrichment + Emirate Dubai marquee page
stages.spec:
STAGE 2 — Pulse Overview enrichment + Emirate Dubai marquee page. binayah-properties only. This is the design-heavy stage; bar is Linear / Stripe / Airbnb dashboards. Designer brief precedes DEV.

## Why this will be good
/pulse/emirate/dubai is the marquee surface — the page a serious investor would screenshot and share. It must read like a Bloomberg market section, not a real-estate listing. Sentiment headline up top sets the tone; the KPI strip teaches in 5 seconds; charts breathe; leaderboards have hover states; the page ends with the WeeklySubscribeForm as the natural next step. Production-ready means: 0/1/many states for every leaderboard; skeleton loaders during data fetch; mobile column-stacking that still looks intentional; the disabled emirate tabs feel like deliberate scaffolding (not broken links). Benchmark: Stripe Atlas dashboard, Linear's insights view, Bloomberg sector pages.

## What DEV must build

### A. Cross-cutting (used by both Overview + Emirate pages)

1. **`PulseEmirateNav` component** at `src/components/pulse/PulseEmirateNav.tsx`:
   - Sub-nav showing 5 emirate tabs: Dubai (active link to `/pulse/emirate/dubai`), Abu Dhabi / Sharjah / Ras Al Khaimah / Ajman (each disabled with a small "Coming soon" badge using the gold accent token). Disabled tabs are non-clickable (cursor-not-allowed, reduced opacity, but still readable per a11y).
   - Sticky on scroll under the main navbar (z-index appropriate; do not collide with `Navbar.tsx`).
   - Mobile: horizontal scroll with snap points; active tab anchored left.
   - Apply DESIGN_SYSTEM.md: pulse-theme tokens since these tabs render on `/pulse` (dark theme). Active tab uses `--pulse-gold` underline; inactive tabs `--pulse-text-muted`.
   - Render at the top of every existing Pulse page: `/pulse`, `/pulse/trending`, `/pulse/calculator`, `/pulse/compare`, `/pulse/guides`, `/pulse/emirate/dubai`.

2. **`SentimentIndicator` component** at `src/components/pulse/SentimentIndicator.tsx`:
   - Props: `{ trend3moMoM: number }` — derived server-side from DldArea aggregate avgPpsf MoM over last 3 months.
   - Output: a labeled chip — Bullish (green) if trend > +1.0%, Bearish (red) if < -1.0%, Neutral (gold) otherwise. Includes a tiny inline sparkline (3 dots or mini line).
   - Tooltip on hover/tap: "Based on Dubai average price/sqft trend over the last 3 months." Uses convention from DESIGN_SYSTEM.md tooltips section.
   - This is a primitive used by Overview top + Emirate page.

3. **`KpiTile` + `KpiStrip` components** at `src/components/pulse/KpiTile.tsx`:
   - Tile: number, label, optional sub-trend (▲ +2.4% MoM in green or ▼ -0.8% in red), optional `lowConfidence` warning icon. Hover: subtle gold border glow (use `pulse-glass-hover` class). On click navigates to a relevant filter (when a target is provided).
   - Strip: responsive grid of 3 / 6 tiles depending on screen.
   - Empty/loading state: skeleton shimmer matching tile shape, NOT a spinner.

4. **`CommunityLeaderboard` component** at `src/components/pulse/CommunityLeaderboard.tsx`:
   - Props: `{ metric: 'pricePsf' | 'yield' | 'yoy' | 'volume'; limit?: number; areas: AreaWithMetrics[] }`.
   - Renders top-N rows with: rank, community name (linked to community page), metric value, MoM trend, mini sparkline.
   - Hover lifts row, shows muted secondary metrics (avg sale, avg rent, transactions YTD).
   - `lowConfidence` rows show a small gold warning icon with tooltip "Limited transaction sample — interpret cautiously."
   - Mobile: vertical stack with metric on right, trend below.
   - Empty state: "No communities meet the threshold yet — check back next week." (specific, not generic).

5. **`MoversTicker` component** at `src/components/pulse/MoversTicker.tsx`:
   - Horizontal scroll strip of 3-5 community cards showing %MoM movers (up + down). Each card: community thumbnail (use `community.image` if available, else gradient placeholder), name, %, mini-sparkline. Click → community page.
   - Auto-scroll on desktop (slow, pause on hover); manual scroll on mobile with snap.
   - Empty: skip the section entirely (don't show "no movers").

6. **`SharePulseButtons` component** at `src/components/pulse/SharePulseButtons.tsx`:
   - Buttons: WhatsApp / X / LinkedIn / Copy link / Save as PDF. Each share URL gets `?utm_source=<network>&utm_medium=share&utm_campaign=pulse-<page>`.
   - WhatsApp / X / LinkedIn open share-sheet URLs that include the OG image (the OG route from stage 1). Copy link uses navigator.clipboard with a toast confirmation.
   - Save as PDF: DISABLED in Stage 2. The /api/pdf/pulse route was removed in Stage 1 (pdfkit + Turbopack incompatibility; reimplemented in Stage 3 with @react-pdf/renderer). In Stage 2, render the PDF button as visually present but disabled (cursor-not-allowed, muted opacity, tooltip "PDF export coming soon") so the layout slot is already wired when Stage 3 enables it.
   - Apply DESIGN_SYSTEM.md icon-button conventions (44px tap, lucide icons). Stage 3 will replace any existing "Embed" buttons with this — but in stage 2 this lives wherever Overview + Trending currently have share affordances.

### B. Overview enrichment — `/pulse` page (`src/app/[locale]/pulse/page.tsx` + client)

7. Above-the-fold: SentimentIndicator → 6-tile KpiStrip (transactions YTD, avg ppsf, avg yield, YoY growth, off-plan share, communities tracked, most-active community). Most-active community KPI shows the community's name as a clickable label.
8. MoversTicker section.
9. Featured insight panel (full-width card): pulls latest News article via `/api/news?limit=1`, shows headline + 2-line excerpt + thumbnail + "Read insight" CTA. SharePulseButtons inline.
10. CommunityLeaderboard with default metric `pricePsf`, top 10, plus a metric switcher (segmented pill: Price / Yield / YoY / Volume).
11. Bottom: WeeklySubscribeForm in card variant, source=`pulse-overview`.

### C. Emirate Dubai page — `src/app/[locale]/pulse/emirate/dubai/page.tsx` + client (THE marquee surface)

12. Hero section: page title "Dubai property pulse" (eyebrow "Emirate report" in gold), one-line summary ("Q-by-quarter health of the Dubai residential market"), SentimentIndicator front and center.
13. KPI strip — same 6 tiles as Overview but Dubai-specific.
14. **24-month avgPpsf trend chart** — Recharts (already in repo or add) line chart, dark-pulse-theme styled, gold accent line, hover tooltip showing month + value. Mobile: keep readable (don't shrink to nothing — minimum 250px height, scroll horizontally if labels collide). Server fetch `/api/dld/areas/dubai/monthly-trend?months=24` (if endpoint doesn't exist, use existing `/api/market-stats` aggregation; DEV decides — endpoint discovery is part of the spec).
15. **Monthly transaction volume bar chart** — companion to (14), bars in `--pulse-green` with hover state.
16. Community highlights — 4 cards: Highest price · Best yield · Fastest growth · Best value (smallest price + decent yield). Each card: community name, headline metric, secondary metric, micro thumbnail. Apply `pulse-glass` styling. Click → community page.
17. Developer highlights — 4 cards: Most active (by transaction count) · Fastest growing (YoY units) · Most off-plan (% of inventory) · Best yield. Compute server-side from existing `Project` collection. If `Project` doesn't carry developer transaction count, compute via aggregation; DEV picks the lightest path.
18. Developer comparison table — top 8 developers, columns: name, projects, off-plan share, avg starting price, latest launch. Use existing tabular pattern; sticky header on scroll, sortable column headers.
19. "Why invest in Dubai" section — 3-up grid of trust-bearing facts (e.g. "0% income tax", "Golden visa pathway", "X% YoY foreign investment growth"). Static curated copy + links to relevant Pulse guides if any. Styled to NOT look like marketing fluff — minimal, factual, citation-style.
20. CommunityLeaderboard with metric switcher (price / yield / YoY / volume), top 10.
21. 3-up featured community cards (curated: e.g. Palm Jumeirah, Dubai Marina, JVC) — luxury card design, hover lifts, click → community page.
22. WeeklySubscribeForm in card variant, source=`pulse-emirate-dubai`. Full-width band before footer.
23. SharePulseButtons in the page header AND a sticky variant on scroll (mobile only? DEV decides — but ensure it doesn't collide with WhatsApp FAB or AI chat widget per the floating elements map).

### D. Compare page leaderboard — `/pulse/compare`
24. Inject CommunityLeaderboard with metric=`yield` near the top so the yield-by-community story is visible at a glance. DEV decides exact placement.

## Design intent (apply DESIGN_PRINCIPLES + DESIGN_SYSTEM)
- "Beauty is structural": typography hierarchy on the emirate page must be precise — eyebrow / h1 / lede / KPIs need real visual rhythm, not 10 elements at the same size.
- "Show the data": community cards show real thumbnails, not heart icons. KPIs show numbers, not bars-without-labels.
- "Interactivity is life": every card hovers; every chart has hover state; metric switcher animates between states; sentiment chip pulses subtly when bullish.
- "Design for scale": leaderboards must work at 100 communities (paginate or limit + "see all"), at 0 (warm empty state), at 1 (no awkward grid).
- "Empty states are features": low-confidence area = warm gold tooltip, never gray-and-vague.
- "Mobile is the default": KPI strip stacks 2x3, charts maintain readable Y axis labels, leaderboards become vertical cards.
- "Use conventions": the metric switcher is a segmented pill group (DESIGN_SYSTEM.md universal conventions), NOT a custom thing.
- Pulse-theme dark tokens used throughout — no `text-foreground` on `--pulse-bg` (the GOTCHA from Apr 18 applies).

## Data sources / endpoints
- `/api/dld/areas` (existing) — community list with metrics.
- `/api/dld/areas/:slug/yield` (NEW from stage 1) — yield + lowConfidence flag.
- `/api/projects` (existing) — for off-plan share + developer aggregations.
- `/api/news?limit=1` (existing) — featured insight.
- `/api/market-stats` or new `/api/dld/areas/dubai/monthly-trend` — DEV picks one for the 24-mo trend chart. If a new endpoint is needed on binayah-api, add it; document in commit.

## Out of scope (stage 2)
- Calculator improvements — stage 3.
- Compare presets buttons — stage 3.
- Replacing "Embed" with "Save PDF" everywhere — stage 3 (but new SharePulseButtons component already lacks an Embed button).
- New project launches list on Trending — stage 3.
- Guide detail layout polish — stage 3.
- Search analytics-style "most searched communities" — out of scope entirely.

## demoPath (QA verification)

5. Visit `http://localhost:3000/pulse/emirate/dubai` → full marquee page renders. SentimentIndicator + KPIs + 24-mo chart + transaction volume + community highlights + developer highlights + comparison table + leaderboard + featured cards + WeeklySubscribeForm + share buttons. No horizontal scroll on 390px.
6. Submit WeeklySubscribeForm with QA email → ConfirmationPending screen replaces the form.
7. Visit `/pulse/compare` → CommunityLeaderboard with yield metric appears.
8. Mobile 390px sweep on emirate page → KPIs stack 2x3, charts remain readable, no clipped tables.

stages.spec:
STAGE 3 — Calculator overhaul + PDF reimpl + Save-as-PDF wiring + sitewide polish. binayah-properties primary; one small binayah-api addition (calculator-lead funnel).

## Context from prior stages (read before coding)
- Compare presets: ALREADY SHIPPED in Stage 2 (CompareClient.tsx has PRESETS const + applyPreset). Stage 3 must only verify preset slugs match what the compare API actually accepts, and update if wrong. No new preset code needed.
- New launches on Trending: ALREADY SHIPPED in Stage 2 (TrendingClient.tsx has newLaunches + LaunchCard). No new launches code needed.
- PDF route: NOT shipped. /api/pdf/pulse does not exist. The DubaiEmirateClient has a disabled PdfDisabledButton placeholder (DubaiEmirateClient.tsx line ~237) with tooltip 'PDF export is coming next week — Stage 3'. Stage 3 must ship the route AND enable the button.
- Calculator: has hardcoded S&P 8%/savings 3% benchmarks, WhatsApp share text (no URL state encoding), no verdict explainer block, no projection lines. All calculator work is new.
- SharePulseButtons: not a standalone component — share logic is inline in each page. Dubai page has WhatsApp + clipboard share inline in DubaiEmirateClient.

## What DEV must build

### A. PDF route — src/app/api/pdf/pulse/route.ts
Use @react-pdf/renderer (NOT pdfkit — incompatible with Next.js 15 Turbopack). Install: npm install @react-pdf/renderer.
- Returns a PDF download: Content-Disposition: attachment; filename="binayah-pulse-<yyyy-mm-dd>.pdf"
- Content: 2 pages. Page 1 — brand header (Binayah Pulse wordmark, date), Dubai KPI strip (transactions YTD, avg ppsf, avg yield, YoY, off-plan share — static/hardcoded fallbacks are fine if live DLD fetch is too slow; use a 60s timeout), top 5 communities table. Page 2 — brief methodology note + binayahhub.com footer.
- Use @react-pdf/renderer Document/Page/Text/View/StyleSheet primitives only. No image fetching over network (avoids timeout issues).
- Cache: Cache-Control: public, max-age=3600 header.
- QA: GET /api/pdf/pulse -> Content-Type: application/pdf, file downloads and opens.

### B. Wire Save-as-PDF button on Dubai page
In src/app/[locale]/pulse/emirate/dubai/DubaiEmirateClient.tsx:
- Replace the PdfDisabledButton component (currently cursor-not-allowed, disabled) with a real clickable button.
- onClick: window.open('/api/pdf/pulse', '_blank') (simplest — triggers browser download).
- Update the i18n key dubaiEmirate.pdfTooltip from 'PDF export is coming next week — Stage 3' to 'Download Dubai market report as PDF'.
- Button must match existing share-row styling (same rounded-lg ghost style as the WhatsApp button next to it).
- Also check the Overview page (/pulse page) and any other pulse page that has a 'Save as PDF' placeholder — wire the same /api/pdf/pulse call.

### C. Calculator overhaul — src/app/[locale]/pulse/calculator/CalculatorClient.tsx

1. Three labeled benchmark lines on results chart:
   - Replace hardcoded sp500Roi and savingsRoi with constants from a new src/lib/calculatorBenchmarks.ts module:
     export const BENCHMARKS = {
       sp500: { label: 'S&P 500 5-yr avg', annualRate: 0.102, source: 'S&P 500 historical total return, 2020-2025' },
       uaeSavings: { label: 'UAE Savings 5-yr avg', annualRate: 0.035, source: 'UAE Central Bank average savings rate, 2020-2025' },
       dubaiProperty: { label: 'Dubai property 5-yr avg', annualRate: 0.074, source: 'DLD transaction data, annualized 5-yr avg' },
     } as const;
   - The Dubai property rate 7.4%/yr is a reasonable hardcoded approximation (avoid async fetch inside the chart component).
   - Each legend chip shows the label + a (i) icon. Hover tooltip shows the source string.
   - On the results chart (Recharts), render 3 reference lines for S&P, UAE Savings, Dubai property using different dash patterns (gray dashed, gray dotted, gold solid).

2. Verdict explainer block — extract to src/components/pulse/CalculatorVerdict.tsx:
   - Export a pure computeVerdict function at module scope:
     export function computeVerdict(rentalYield: number, dubaiAvgYield: number, fiveYrForecast: number, vsSp500Delta: number): 'strong' | 'moderate' | 'weak' {
       // 1pt: yield > dubaiAvgYield, 1pt: fiveYrForecast > 5%, 1pt: vsSp500Delta > 0
       const score = (rentalYield > dubaiAvgYield ? 1 : 0) + (fiveYrForecast > 5 ? 1 : 0) + (vsSp500Delta > 0 ? 1 : 0);
       return score >= 3 ? 'strong' : score >= 2 ? 'moderate' : 'weak';
     }
   - Use dubaiAvgYield = 6.8 as the hardcoded Dubai avg.
   - Render 3-row block below chart: 'Rental yield: X% (vs Dubai avg 6.8%)', '5-yr appreciation forecast: Z%', 'vs S&P 500 5-yr: +-W%'. Color-coded.
   - Replace existing single verdict badge with this block + Verdict pill (Strong=green, Moderate=gold, Weak=red).

3. 3-yr and 5-yr projection lines — extend the Recharts chart:
   - Add two dotted reference lines extending beyond the user's selected horizon.
   - Small footnote below chart: 'Projected: linear extrapolation based on DLD historical trend. Not a guarantee.' (i18n key calculator.projectionDisclaimer)

4. Calculator share — URL state encoding:
   - On calc inputs change, update window.history.replaceState with querystring: ?price=<n>&downpct=<n>&years=<n>&community=<slug>&monthlyRent=<n>.
   - On page mount, read useSearchParams() and seed initial state from querystring if present.
   - Existing WhatsApp share button: update share text to 3-line format: 'Looking at AED <price> in <community>. Calc says <yield>% yield, +<appreciation>% 5-yr appreciation. <url>'.
   - Add a 'Copy link' icon button next to WhatsApp (clipboard.writeText with toast). Reuse lucide Copy icon + existing toast pattern.

5. Calculator email — new src/components/pulse/CalculatorEmailModal.tsx + src/app/api/calculator/email/route.ts:
   - 'Email me this report' button below verdict block.
   - Modal: name (required) + email (required) + phone (optional). Submit button 'Send report'.
   - /api/calculator/email route: (a) sends HTML email with calc summary, (b) POSTs to ${API_BASE_URL}/api/market-report/subscribe with { email, name, phone, intents: ['calculator-lead'], source: 'calculator', areas: [community] }.
   - Note: Fastify endpoint already accepts intents: ['calculator-lead'] (open enum from Stage 1). No binayah-api change needed.
   - On success: close modal, show toast 'Sent to <email> — check your inbox'.

### D. Compare presets — verify only
In src/app/[locale]/pulse/compare/CompareClient.tsx:
- Stage 2 shipped PRESETS with display-name slugs ('Dubai Marina', 'Palm Jumeirah', etc). The compare table sources data from /api/dld/areas which uses these same display-name keys.
- Verify the 4 presets work end-to-end (click -> table populates). If any preset slug returns empty (silent failure), update the slug.
- Update preset slugs to match original spec intent where possible: Waterfront Luxury -> ['Palm Jumeirah', 'Dubai Marina', 'Bluewaters Island'] if Bluewaters Island exists in DLD data, else keep current. High Yield -> ['JVC', 'Dubai Sports City', 'Dubai South'] if those exist, else keep current. This is a verification + minor slug-correction task, not a rewrite.

### E. Share buttons UTM audit
- DubaiEmirateClient.tsx share URLs already have utm_source but use hardcoded utm_campaign=pulse-2026-04. Update to: utm_campaign=pulse-emirate-dubai.
- Check the Overview page (/pulse) and Trending page for share buttons. Ensure all have utm_source, utm_medium=share, utm_campaign=pulse-<page-slug>.
- Calculator share URLs (new from section C.4) must also include UTM params.

### F. Guide detail layout polish — bounded scope
In src/app/[locale]/pulse/guides/[slug]/GuideDetailClient.tsx:
- Add CSS heading hierarchy to guide content render: h1 renders larger than h2 renders larger than h3. Use Tailwind prose classes or explicit heading size overrides.
- Related-areas footer: 3-card grid of top 3 communities from /api/dld/areas?limit=3 (or static curated list: Dubai Marina, Downtown Dubai, JVC). Cards show community name + link to /communities/<slug>.
- Explicitly SKIP embedded stats blocks (too much scope for diff cap). Add a TODO comment in the file.

## Out of scope (do not add)
- Embed widget feature.
- New launches on Trending (already shipped in Stage 2).
- Other emirate pages.
- Search analytics.
- Embedded stats blocks in guides (deferred with TODO comment).

## demoPath (QA verification)
1. GET /api/pdf/pulse -> Content-Type: application/pdf, file downloads, 2-page Binayah Pulse PDF.
2. Visit /pulse/emirate/dubai hero share row -> 'Save as PDF' button is now clickable (not disabled). Click -> PDF downloads.
3. Visit /pulse/calculator -> enter AED 2M, 25% down, 25 yrs, Dubai Marina. Results chart shows 3 labeled benchmark lines (S&P dashed gray, UAE Savings dotted gray, Dubai property gold solid). Each legend chip shows (i) icon.
4. Hover S&P legend chip -> tooltip shows 'S&P 500 historical total return, 2020-2025'.

8. Click WhatsApp share -> text includes 3-line preview with yield + appreciation + URL.
9. Click 'Email me this report' -> modal opens with name/email/phone. Submit with QA email -> toast confirms.

## filesTouched (expected)

- src/app/[locale]/pulse/emirate/dubai/DubaiEmirateClient.tsx (edit — enable PDF button + UTM fix)

plannerOutput.taskSummary: Pulse Property roadmap — 17 deliverables across 4 batches reorganized into 3 stages: backend foundation + subscription, Pulse Overview + Emirate Dubai, Calculator + polish.
plannerOutput.confirmedScope:
Build out Pulse Property roadmap — 17 deliverables across 3 stages (single iterate, --stages=3 --max=3).

STAGE 1 — Backend foundation + Subscription system (binayah-api + binayah-properties)
1. New MarketReportSubscription Mongoose model on binayah-api: email, name, phone (opt), intents[] (buy/rent/sell/invest), areas[], propertyTypes[], budgetMin, budgetMax, confirmedAt, unsubscribedAt, lastSentAt, unsubToken (random), createdAt
2. Endpoints on binayah-api:
   - POST /api/market-report/subscribe — store sub, fire confirmation email (double opt-in)
   - GET  /api/market-report/confirm/:token — set confirmedAt
   - GET  /api/market-report/unsubscribe/:token — set unsubscribedAt
   - POST /api/admin/market-report/send-weekly — admin trigger to send digest now (also wired to a Render cron job at Mon 09:00 UTC)
3. Email service helper (binayah-api): uses existing SMTP_* env vars; sends confirmation + weekly digest with personalized content (top movers in user's areas, new launches matching property/budget, 1 featured guide). Plain HTML, unsub link in footer.
4. Yield-by-area aggregation endpoint on binayah-api: GET /api/dld/areas/:slug/yield — computes avg rent / avg sale per area; flag samples <50 with `lowConfidence:true`.
5. OG image route on binayah-properties: /api/og/pulse — Next.js route returning a PNG card with Pulse headline metric, used for share unfurls.
6. PDF render route on binayah-properties: /api/pdf/pulse — server-renders a Pulse summary PDF (uses puppeteer-core or pdfkit; pick whichever is lighter).
7. WeeklySubscribeForm component on binayah-properties: 1-step form with name+email required + optional phone + intent chips + areas multiselect (autocomplete from /api/communities) + property type chips + budget range; replaces every existing "Get a free market report" form (currently on /pulse, /pulse/trending, /pulse/emirate, etc.). Confirmation-pending screen after submit.
8. /account/market-reports page (logged-in users) to manage their subscription preferences.

STAGE 2 — Pulse Overview enrichment + Emirate Dubai detail page
9. Sub-nav with Emirate tabs on every Pulse page: Dubai active, AUH/SHJ/RAK/Ajman with "Coming soon" badges + disabled links.
10. Sentiment indicator (Bullish / Neutral / Bearish) — derive from 3-month MoM avgPpsf trend; appears on Overview top + Emirate page.
11. Most-active-community KPI on Overview — read DldArea sorted by totalSales desc, take top.
12. Top-communities leaderboard on Overview + Emirate (price/sqft, yield, YoY, volume) — uses existing /api/dld/areas with new yield endpoint from stage 1.
13. Quick ticker on Overview (top 3 movers, horizontal scroll, links to communities) — reuses Trending movers logic.
14. Off-plan share KPI on Overview + Emirate — compute % of Project where status is off-plan.
15. /pulse/emirate/dubai page — sentiment, KPI strip (transactions YTD, avg ppsf, avg yield, YoY growth, off-plan share, communities tracked), 24-mo price trend chart, monthly transaction volume chart, community highlights (highest price / best yield / fastest growth / best value), developer highlights (most active / fastest growing / most off-plan / best yield), developer comparison table, "why invest in Dubai" section, community leaderboard, featured community cards, lead form using new WeeklySubscribeForm.
16. Yield-by-community leaderboard on Compare + Emirate — uses stage 1 endpoint; shows lowConfidence flag for thin samples.
17. Featured insight panel + share buttons on Trending + Overview — pulls latest News article; share buttons (WhatsApp, X, LinkedIn, Copy Link) wire OG image; UTM tags on every share URL.

STAGE 3 — Calculator overhaul + Compare presets + Polish
18. Calculator benchmarks — replace hardcoded S&P/Savings with three labeled lines: "S&P 500 5-yr avg" (10.2%), "UAE Savings 5-yr avg" (3.5%), "Dubai property 5-yr avg" (real, derived from DLD MoM trend); each gets a tooltip with source.
19. Calculator verdict — replace single "Strong Investment" badge with explainer block: "Rental yield: X% (vs Dubai avg Y%)", "5-yr appreciation forecast: Z%", "vs S&P 500 5-yr: ±W%". Verdict pill (Strong/Moderate/Weak) driven by explicit scoring rule documented in code.
20. Calculator share — Share button copies URL with calc state encoded in querystring; recipient lands on filled-in calc. WhatsApp share includes 3-line preview text.
21. Calculator email — POST /api/calculator/email on binayah-properties → server-side HTML email with full calc result; lead logged to MarketReportSubscription as a "calculator-lead" intent.
22. Calculator projection lines — add 3-yr and 5-yr projection lines on the results chart, linear extrapolation from DLD trend with disclaimer.
23. Compare presets — 4 curated arrays: "Waterfront Luxury" (Palm Jumeirah, Dubai Marina, Bluewaters Island), "High Yield" (JVC, Dubai Sports City, Dubai South), "Urban Core" (Downtown Dubai, Business Bay, DIFC), "Emerging Stars" (Dubai Hills Estate, Tilal Al Ghaf, Town Square). Click loads them into the comparison.
24. New project launches list on Trending — Project.find({}).sort({launchDate: -1}).limit(6) with developer, area, starting price, payment plan.
25. Share buttons cleanup site-wide — replace "Embed" with "Save as PDF" (calls /api/pdf/pulse from stage 1); add UTM tagging on every share URL across Pulse pages.
26. Guide detail layout polish — heading hierarchy, embedded stats blocks pulling from DLD API by mentioned area, related-areas footer with placeKind links.

CONSTRAINTS
- No multi-emirate data exists yet — only Dubai pipelines work. Other emirate tabs MUST be visible-but-disabled with "Coming soon" badges. Don't fake numbers.
- All new UI strings must be in en/ar/zh/ru locale files (eslint i18next/no-literal-string blocks Vercel build otherwise).
- New env vars (none expected — SMTP_* + AWS_* + MONGODB_URI already on Render) — flag if any new ones are needed.
- Render cron for weekly send: register in render.yaml as a cronJob service (existing pattern: dld-refresh runs daily 03:00 UTC).
- Email service uses existing SMTP_HOST/PORT/USER/PASS already configured for inquiries.
- Existing "Get a free market report" form must be REPLACED, not duplicated, so we don't end up with two competing forms on /pulse.
- All routes must be locale-aware (use @/navigation, not next/link directly).
- The /pulse/emirate/dubai page should be the marquee piece — match the design quality bar from .claude/DESIGN_PRINCIPLES.md if it exists.

decisions.stages: 3 sequential stages — backend first (data + endpoints), then UI consumers (Overview + Emirate Dubai), then Calculator + polish.
decisions.subscription: Double opt-in. Confirmation email then weekly Monday digest. Personalised by user areas + property type + budget.
decisions.weekly_cron: Register render cron job at Mon 09:00 UTC, calls /api/admin/market-report/send-weekly with admin secret.
decisions.weekly_form_replaces: All existing get-free-market-report forms across /pulse pages — single shared component.
decisions.emirate_data: Only Dubai. Other emirates visible but disabled with Coming Soon badges. Never fake numbers.
decisions.verdict_scoring: Calculator scoring rule must be explicit in code with a single decision function — not a black box.
decisions.i18n: Every new visible string in en/ar/zh/ru. ESLint i18next/no-literal-string is enforced.

constraints: Wall clock 45 min — total stages must each fit ~12 min DEV + 3 min REVIEW + 5 min QA.

outOfScope: Other emirates (AUH/SHJ/RAK/Ajman) — UI shows Coming Soon only.
Search analytics (most searched communities) — needs tracking pixel; not in scope.
Real Embed widget — replaced with Save PDF instead.

   - The Dubai property rate 7.4%/yr is a reasonable hardcoded approximation (avoid async fetch inside the chart component).

   - Export a pure computeVerdict function at module scope:
     export function computeVerdict(rentalYield: number, dubaiAvgYield: number, fiveYrForecast: number, vsSp500Delta: number): 'strong' | 'moderate' | 'weak' {
       // 1pt: yield > dubaiAvgYield, 1pt: fiveYrForecast > 5%, 1pt: vsSp500Delta > 0
       const score = (rentalYield > dubaiAvgYield ? 1 : 0) + (fiveYrForecast > 5 ? 1 : 0) + (vsSp500Delta > 0 ? 1 : 0);
       return score >= 3 ? 'strong' : score >= 2 ? 'moderate' : 'weak';
     }

+"use client";
+
+import { useState, useMemo, useRef } from "react";
+import { useTranslations } from "next-intl";
+import {
+  AreaChart, Area, BarChart, Bar, XAxis, YAxis, Tooltip, ResponsiveContainer, CartesianGrid, Cell,
+} from "recharts";
+import {
+  ArrowUpRight, TrendingUp, Building2, Percent, Activity, BarChart3, Globe, FileDown,
+  Copy, CheckCheck, Share2,
+} from "lucide-react";
+import Image from "next/image";
+import { motion } from "framer-motion";
+import PulseSentimentChip from "@/components/PulseSentimentChip";
+import CommunityLeaderboard, { type LeaderboardRow } from "@/components/CommunityLeaderboard";
+import FeaturedInsightPanel from "@/components/FeaturedInsightPanel";
+import WeeklySubscribeForm from "@/components/WeeklySubscribeForm";
+import { apiUrl } from "@/lib/api";
+import { useEffect } from "react";
+
+// ── Types ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
+
+interface CommunityStats {
+  area: string;
+  avgPricePerSqft: number;
+  avgSalePrice: number;
+  avgRentPrice: number;
+  rentalYield: number;
+  totalListings: number;
+  offPlanCount: number;
+  secondaryCount: number;
+  investmentScore: number;
+}
+
+interface MarketStats {
+  summary: { avgPricePerSqft: number; totalListings: number; avgYield: number; offPlanShare: number; offPlanCount: number; secondaryCount: number };
+  priceByArea: { area: string; price: number; count: number }[];
+  yieldByArea: { area: string; yield: number }[];
+  communityMatrix: CommunityStats[];
+}
+
+interface MarketData {
+  transactions: {
+    hasData: boolean;
+    summary: { totalTransactions: number; totalValue: number; avgPpsf: number; avgTransactionValue: number };
+    monthly: { label: string; count: number; avgPpsf: number; totalValue: number }[];
+    byArea: { area: string; count: number; totalValue: number; avgPrice: number; avgPpsf: number }[];
+  } | null;
+  news: { title: string; url: string; source: string; summary: string; imageUrl: string; publishedAt: string }[];
+}
+
+interface Project {
+  slug: string;
+  name: string;
+  developer?: string;
+  status?: string;
+  propertyType?: string;
+  createdAt?: string;
+}
+
+interface Props {
+  marketStats: MarketStats | null;
+  marketData: MarketData | null;
+  areasData: { results?: { area: string; totalSales: number; avgPpsf: number }[] } | null;
+  projectsData: { results?: Project[] } | null;
+}
+
+// ── Design tokens ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
+const GOLD = "hsl(43, 55%, 55%)";
+const GREEN = "hsl(160, 55%, 42%)";
+const TOOLTIP_STYLE = {
+  borderRadius: 10,
+  border: "1px solid hsl(40,15%,88%)",
+  fontSize: 12,
+  boxShadow: "0 4px 16px rgba(0,0,0,0.08)",
+};
+
+// ── Helpers ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
+const AED = (n: number) => {
+  if (n >= 1_000_000_000) return `AED ${(n / 1_000_000_000).toFixed(1)}B`;
+  if (n >= 1_000_000) return `AED ${(n / 1_000_000).toFixed(1)}M`;
+  if (n >= 1_000) return `AED ${(n / 1_000).toFixed(0)}K`;
+  return `AED ${n.toLocaleString()}`;
+};
+
+// ── KPI Strip Card ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
+function KpiCard({
+  label, value, sub, icon: Icon, gold = false,
+}: {
+  label: string; value: string | number; sub?: string; icon: React.ElementType; gold?: boolean;
+}) {
+  return (
+    <div
+      className="rounded-xl border p-4 bg-card"
+      style={{ borderColor: gold ? GOLD : undefined }}
+    >
+      <div className="flex items-center justify-between mb-2">
+        <p className="text-[10px] font-semibold uppercase tracking-[0.15em] text-muted-foreground">{label}</p>
+        <div className={`w-7 h-7 rounded-lg flex items-center justify-center ${gold ? "bg-amber-50" : "bg-muted/50"}`}>
+          <Icon className="h-3.5 w-3.5" style={{ color: gold ? GOLD : "hsl(var(--muted-foreground))" }} />
+        </div>
+      </div>
+      <p className="text-2xl font-bold text-foreground tabular-nums" style={{ fontVariantNumeric: "tabular-nums" }}>
+        {value}
+      </p>
+      {sub && <p className="text-[10px] text-muted-foreground mt-1">{sub}</p>}
+    </div>
+  );
+}
+
+// ── Why Dubai typography entry ────────────────────────────────────────────────
+function WhyEntry({ num, headline, sub, source }: { num: string; headline: string; sub: string; source: string }) {
+  return (
+    <div className="flex gap-5">
+      <span
+        className="text-3xl font-bold flex-shrink-0 mt-1 tabular-nums font-mono"
+        style={{ color: GOLD, fontVariantNumeric: "tabular-nums" }}
+      >
+        {num}
+      </span>
+      <div>
+        <p className="text-xl font-bold text-foreground leading-snug mb-1">{headline}</p>
+        <p className="text-sm text-muted-foreground leading-relaxed mb-1.5">{sub}</p>
+        <p className="text-[10px] text-muted-foreground/50 italic">{source}</p>
+      </div>
+    </div>
+  );
+}
+
+// ── Highlight card (4-up) ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────
+function HighlightCard({
+  eyebrow, title, value, sub, href,
+}: {
+  eyebrow: string; title: string; value: string; sub?: string; href?: string;
+}) {
+  return (
+    <div className="group relative bg-card border border-border/50 rounded-xl p-5 hover:border-accent/60 hover:-translate-y-0.5 hover:shadow-md transition-all">
+      <p
+        className="text-[9px] font-bold tracking-[0.3em] uppercase mb-1"
+        style={{ color: GOLD }}
+      >
+        {eyebrow}
+      </p>
+      <p className="text-sm font-bold text-foreground line-clamp-1 mb-2">{title}</p>
+      <p className="text-2xl font-bold text-foreground tabular-nums" style={{ fontVariantNumeric: "tabular-nums" }}>{value}</p>
+      {sub && <p className="text-[10px] text-muted-foreground mt-1">{sub}</p>}
+      {href && (
+        <a
+          href={href}
+          className="absolute top-4 right-4 text-muted-foreground/30 group-hover:text-accent transition-colors"
+          aria-label={`View ${title}`}
+        >
+          <ArrowUpRight className="h-4 w-4" />
+        </a>
+      )}
+    </div>
+  );
+}
+
+// ── Featured community card ───────────────────────────────────────────────────
+const FEATURED_COMMUNITIES = [
+  { slug: "palm-jumeirah", name: "Palm Jumeirah", imageUrl: "/images/communities/palm-jumeirah.jpg", tagline: "Ultra-luxury waterfront icon" },
+  { slug: "dubai-marina", name: "Dubai Marina", imageUrl: "/images/communities/dubai-marina.jpg", tagline: "Vibrant urban waterfront" },
+  { slug: "jumeirah-village-circle", name: "JVC", imageUrl: "/images/communities/jvc.jpg", tagline: "Highest-yield community" },
+  { slug: "dubai-hills-estate", name: "Dubai Hills Estate", imageUrl: "/images/communities/dubai-hills.jpg", tagline: "Green master-plan living" },
+  { slug: "downtown-dubai", name: "Downtown Dubai", imageUrl: "/images/communities/downtown.jpg", tagline: "The city's epicentre" },
+];
+
+function FeaturedCommunityCard({ community }: { community: typeof FEATURED_COMMUNITIES[0] }) {
+  return (
+    <a
+      href={`/communities/${community.slug}`}
+      className="group relative flex-shrink-0 rounded-2xl overflow-hidden"
+      style={{ width: 280, height: 360, scrollSnapAlign: "start" }}
+    >
+      {/* Full-bleed image */}
+      <div className="absolute inset-0">
+        <Image
+          src={community.imageUrl}
+          alt={community.name}
+          fill
+          className="object-cover transition-transform duration-[600ms] group-hover:scale-105"
+          sizes="280px"
+          unoptimized
+        />
+      </div>
+      {/* Dark gradient overlay */}
+      <div
+        className="absolute inset-0"
+        style={{ background: "linear-gradient(to top, rgba(0,0,0,0.75) 0%, rgba(0,0,0,0.2) 60%, transparent 100%)" }}
+      />
+      {/* Text */}
+      <div className="absolute bottom-0 left-0 right-0 p-5">
+        <p
+          className="text-[9px] font-bold tracking-[0.25em] uppercase mb-1"
+          style={{ color: "hsl(43, 55%, 65%)" }}
+        >
+          {community.tagline}
+        </p>
+        <p className="text-lg font-bold text-white">{community.name}</p>
+      </div>
+    </a>
+  );
+}
+
+// ── Share button (PDF disabled) ───────────────────────────────────────────────
+function PdfDisabledButton({ t }: { t: ReturnType<typeof useTranslations<"dubaiEmirate">> }) {
+  return (
+    <span
+      title={t("pdfTooltip")}
+      className="inline-flex items-center gap-1.5 px-3 py-1.5 rounded-lg border border-border/30 text-xs font-medium text-muted-foreground/40 cursor-not-allowed select-none"
+    >
+      <FileDown className="h-3.5 w-3.5" />
+      {t("pdfButton")}
+    </span>
+  );
+}
+
+// ── Main component ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
+export default function DubaiEmirateClient({ marketStats, marketData, areasData, projectsData }: Props) {
+  const t = useTranslations("dubaiEmirate");
+  const [shareUrl, setShareUrl] = useState("");
+  const [copied, setCopied] = useState(false);
+  const [yieldRows, setYieldRows] = useState<{ area: string; yieldPct: number; avgRentPerSqft: number; avgSalePerSqft: number; lowConfidence: boolean }[]>([]);
+  const communityScrollRef = useRef<HTMLDivElement>(null);
+
+  // Fetch yield data for top communities
+  useEffect(() => {
+    const topAreas = marketStats?.communityMatrix?.slice(0, 10) ?? [];
+    if (topAreas.length === 0) return;
+    const slugs = topAreas.map((c) => c.area.toLowerCase().replace(/\s+/g, "-").replace(/[^a-z0-9-]/g, ""));
+    Promise.all(
+      slugs.map((slug) =>
+        fetch(apiUrl(`/api/dld/areas/${slug}/yield`))
+          .then((r) => r.ok ? r.json() : null)
+          .catch(() => null)

+  const sortedByScore = [...matrix].sort((a, b) => b.investmentScore - a.investmentScore);

+import Navbar from "@/components/Navbar";
+import Footer from "@/components/Footer";

+import PulseEmirateNav from "@/components/PulseEmirateNav";

+import { serverApiUrl, serverFetch } from "@/lib/api";
+import { getTranslations } from "next-intl/server";
+import type { Metadata } from "next";
+
+export const revalidate = 600;
+
+export const metadata: Metadata = {
+  title: "Dubai Market Report | Binayah Properties",
+  description:
+    "Live Dubai real estate analytics — transactions YTD, average PPSF, rental yield, community leaders, developer rankings, and investment highlights.",
+  openGraph: {
+    title: "Dubai Market Report | Binayah Pulse",
+    description: "Live Dubai real estate analytics — transactions, yield, community highlights.",
+    images: ["/api/og/pulse?metric=Avg+PPSF&trend=up"],
+  },
+};
+
+async function fetchJson(path: string, ms = 12_000) {
+  try {
+    const res = await serverFetch(serverApiUrl(path), ms);
+    if (!res.ok) return null;
+    return res.json();
+  } catch {
+    return null;
+  }
+}
+
+export default async function DubaiEmiratePage() {
+  const t = await getTranslations("dubaiEmirate");
+
+  const [marketStats, marketData, areasData, projectsData] = await Promise.all([
+    fetchJson("/api/market-stats"),
+    fetchJson("/api/market-data"),
+    fetchJson("/api/dld/areas?sort=totalSales&limit=50"),
+    fetchJson("/api/projects?status=active&limit=100"),
+  ]);
+
+  return (
+    <div className="min-h-screen bg-background">
+      <Navbar />
+      <PulseEmirateNav />
+
+      {/* Hero */}
+      <section
+        className="relative pt-32 pb-20 text-white overflow-hidden"
+        style={{ background: "linear-gradient(135deg, #0B3D2E, #1A7A5A)" }}
+      >
+        <div
+          className="absolute inset-0 opacity-[0.04]"
+          style={{
+            backgroundImage: "radial-gradient(circle at 1px 1px, currentColor 1px, transparent 0)",
+            backgroundSize: "48px 48px",
+          }}
+        />
+        <div className="max-w-7xl mx-auto px-4 sm:px-6 relative">
+          {/* Eyebrow */}
+          <p
+            className="text-[10px] font-bold tracking-[0.4em] uppercase mb-3"
+            style={{ color: "hsl(43, 55%, 65%)" }}
+          >
+            {t("eyebrow")}
+          </p>
+
+          {/* Emirate name + sentiment inline */}
+          <div className="flex flex-wrap items-center gap-4 mb-3">
+            <h1 className="text-4xl sm:text-5xl lg:text-6xl font-bold text-white">
+              {t("title")} <span className="italic font-light">{t("titleItalic")}</span>
+            </h1>
+          </div>
+
+          <p className="text-white/70 max-w-2xl text-base sm:text-lg mb-6">
+            {t("lede")}
+          </p>
+
+          {/* Share row */}
+          <div className="flex flex-wrap items-center gap-3 text-xs text-white/60">
+            <div className="flex items-center gap-1.5">
+              <div className="w-1.5 h-1.5 rounded-full bg-emerald-400 animate-pulse" />
+              {t("liveData")}
+            </div>
+            <span className="text-white/30">·</span>
+            <span>{t("dldSource")}</span>
+          </div>
+        </div>
+      </section>
+
+      <DubaiEmirateClient
+        marketStats={marketStats}
+        marketData={marketData}
+        areasData={areasData}
+        projectsData={projectsData}
+      />
+
+      <Footer />
+      <WhatsAppButton />
+    </div>
+  );
+}

+"use client";
+
+import { useState } from "react";
+import { useTranslations } from "next-intl";
+import { ChevronRight } from "lucide-react";
+
+export interface LeaderboardRow {
+  rank: number;
+  area: string;
+  // Primary metrics
+  totalSales: number;
+  ppsf: number;
+  yieldPct: number;
+  yoyPct: number;
+  volume: number;
+  // Secondary (revealed on hover)
+  avgDealSize?: number;
+  offPlanShare?: number;
+  rentAvg?: number;
+}
+
+type MetricKey = "sales" | "ppsf" | "yield" | "yoy" | "volume";
+
+const METRICS: { id: MetricKey; labelKey: string }[] = [
+  { id: "sales", labelKey: "metricSales" },
+  { id: "ppsf", labelKey: "metricPpsf" },
+  { id: "yield", labelKey: "metricYield" },
+  { id: "yoy", labelKey: "metricYoy" },
+  { id: "volume", labelKey: "metricVolume" },
+];
+
+const GOLD = "hsl(43, 55%, 55%)";
+const GREEN = "hsl(160, 55%, 42%)";
+const RED = "hsl(0, 72%, 51%)";
+
+function getMetricValue(row: LeaderboardRow, metric: MetricKey): number {
+  switch (metric) {
+    case "sales": return row.totalSales;
+    case "ppsf": return row.ppsf;
+    case "yield": return row.yieldPct;
+    case "yoy": return row.yoyPct;
+    case "volume": return row.volume;
+  }
+}
+
+function formatMetric(value: number, metric: MetricKey): string {
+  if (value === 0) return "—";
+  switch (metric) {
+    case "sales": return value.toLocaleString();
+    case "ppsf": return `${value.toLocaleString()}`;
+    case "yield": return `${value.toFixed(1)}%`;
+    case "yoy": {
+      const abs = Math.abs(value).toFixed(1);
+      return value >= 0 ? `+${abs}%` : `−${abs}%`;
+    }
+    case "volume": {
+      if (value >= 1_000_000_000) return `AED ${(value / 1_000_000_000).toFixed(1)}B`;
+      if (value >= 1_000_000) return `AED ${(value / 1_000_000).toFixed(1)}M`;
+      return `AED ${(value / 1_000).toFixed(0)}K`;
+    }
+  }
+}
+
+function metricColor(value: number, metric: MetricKey): string | undefined {
+  if (metric === "yoy") {
+    return value > 0 ? GREEN : value < 0 ? RED : undefined;
+  }
+  if (metric === "yield") {
+    return value >= 7 ? GREEN : value >= 5 ? GOLD : undefined;
+  }
+  return undefined;
+}
+
+interface Props {
+  rows: LeaderboardRow[];
+  /** Show a "View all" footer link */
+  viewAllHref?: string;
+}
+
+export default function CommunityLeaderboard({ rows, viewAllHref }: Props) {
+  const t = useTranslations("communityLeaderboard");
+  const [activeMetric, setActiveMetric] = useState<MetricKey>("sales");
+  const [hoveredRow, setHoveredRow] = useState<string | null>(null);
+
+  if (rows.length === 0) {
+    return (
+      <div className="bg-muted/20 border border-border/30 rounded-xl p-8 text-center">
+        <p className="text-sm text-muted-foreground">{t("empty")}</p>
+      </div>
+    );
+  }
+
+  // Sort rows by active metric (desc for most metrics, except yoy/yield we still rank by value desc)
+  const sorted = [...rows].sort((a, b) => {
+    const av = getMetricValue(a, activeMetric);
+    const bv = getMetricValue(b, activeMetric);
+    return bv - av;
+  });
+
+  const maxVal = Math.max(...sorted.map((r) => getMetricValue(r, activeMetric)), 1);
+
+  return (
+    <div className="space-y-3">
+      {/* Segmented pill metric switcher */}
+      <div
+        role="group"
+        aria-label={t("metricSwitcherLabel")}
+        className="flex gap-1 flex-wrap"
+      >
+        {METRICS.map((m) => (
+          <button
+            key={m.id}
+            onClick={() => setActiveMetric(m.id)}
+            className={`px-3 py-1.5 rounded-full text-xs font-semibold transition-all border ${
+              activeMetric === m.id
+                ? "text-white border-transparent shadow-sm"
+                : "text-muted-foreground border-border/50 bg-card hover:border-accent/40 hover:text-foreground"
+            }`}
+            style={
+              activeMetric === m.id
+                ? { background: "linear-gradient(135deg, #0B3D2E, #1A7A5A)" }
+                : undefined
+            }
+          >
+            {t(m.labelKey as Parameters<typeof t>[0])}
+          </button>
+        ))}
+      </div>
+
+      {/* Linear-dense rows with 1px dividers */}
+      <div className="bg-card border border-border/50 rounded-xl overflow-hidden">
+        {sorted.map((row, idx) => {
+          const isFirst = idx === 0;
+          const val = getMetricValue(row, activeMetric);
+          const barWidth = maxVal > 0 ? Math.min((val / maxVal) * 100, 100) : 0;
+          const valueColor = metricColor(val, activeMetric);
+          const isHovered = hoveredRow === row.area;
+
+          return (
+            <div
+              key={row.area}
+              onMouseEnter={() => setHoveredRow(row.area)}
+              onMouseLeave={() => setHoveredRow(null)}
+              className={`relative flex items-center gap-3 px-4 py-2.5 transition-colors ${
+                idx < sorted.length - 1 ? "border-b border-border/40" : ""
+              } ${isHovered ? "bg-muted/30" : ""}`}
+              style={{ minHeight: 44 }}
+            >
+              {/* Rank */}
+              <div className="w-6 flex-shrink-0 text-center">
+                {isFirst ? (
+                  <span
+                    className="inline-flex items-center justify-center w-5 h-5 rounded-full text-[10px] font-bold text-white"
+                    style={{ background: GOLD }}
+                  >
+                    1
+                  </span>
+                ) : (
+                  <span className="text-xs text-muted-foreground/50 font-medium">{idx + 1}</span>
+                )}
+              </div>
+
+              {/* Area name */}
+              <div className="flex-1 min-w-0">
+                <p className={`text-sm font-medium truncate ${isFirst ? "text-foreground" : "text-foreground/80"}`}>
+                  {row.area}
+                </p>
+
+                {/* Bar */}
+                <div className="h-1 mt-1 rounded-full overflow-hidden bg-muted/60 w-24">
+                  <div
+                    className="h-full rounded-full transition-all duration-500"
+                    style={{
+                      width: `${barWidth}%`,
+                      background: isFirst ? GOLD : "hsl(168, 60%, 30%)",
+                    }}
+                  />
+                </div>
+              </div>
+
+              {/* Primary metric value */}
+              <div className="text-right flex-shrink-0 min-w-[72px]">
+                <p
+                  className="text-sm font-bold tabular-nums"
+                  style={{ color: valueColor ?? (isFirst ? GOLD : "hsl(var(--foreground))"), fontVariantNumeric: "tabular-nums" }}
+                >
+                  {formatMetric(val, activeMetric)}
+                </p>
+              </div>
+
+              {/* Secondary metrics — slide-in on hover */}
+              {isHovered && (
+                <div className="absolute inset-y-0 right-0 flex items-center gap-3 px-4 bg-card/95 backdrop-blur-sm border-l border-border/50 z-10 animate-in slide-in-from-right-4 duration-150">
+                  {row.avgDealSize !== undefined && row.avgDealSize > 0 && (
+                    <div className="text-right">
+                      <p className="text-[10px] text-muted-foreground">{t("secAvgDeal")}</p>
+                      <p className="text-xs font-semibold text-foreground tabular-nums">
+                        AED {(row.avgDealSize / 1_000_000).toFixed(1)}M
+                      </p>
+                    </div>
+                  )}
+                  {row.offPlanShare !== undefined && (
+                    <div className="text-right">
+                      <p className="text-[10px] text-muted-foreground">{t("secOffPlan")}</p>
+                      <p className="text-xs font-semibold text-foreground tabular-nums">
+                        {row.offPlanShare.toFixed(0)}%
+                      </p>
+                    </div>
+                  )}
+                  {row.rentAvg !== undefined && row.rentAvg > 0 && (
+                    <div className="text-right">
+                      <p className="text-[10px] text-muted-foreground">{t("secRentAvg")}</p>
+                      <p className="text-xs font-semibold text-foreground tabular-nums">
+                        AED {(row.rentAvg / 1_000).toFixed(0)}K
+                      </p>
+                    </div>
+                  )}
+                  <ChevronRight className="h-3 w-3 text-muted-foreground/40" />
+                </div>
+              )}
+            </div>
+          );
+        })}
+      </div>
+
+      {viewAllHref && (
+        <div className="text-center pt-1">
+          <a
+            href={viewAllHref}
+            className="text-xs font-medium text-muted-foreground hover:text-foreground transition-colors"
+          >
+            {t("viewAll")} →
+          </a>
+        </div>
+      )}
+    </div>
+  );
+}

+"use client";
+
+import { useTranslations } from "next-intl";
+import Image from "next/image";
+import { ExternalLink, Share2, Copy, CheckCheck } from "lucide-react";
+import { useState } from "react";
+
+export interface InsightArticle {
+  title: string;
+  url: string;
+  source: string;
+  summary: string;
+  imageUrl: string;
+  publishedAt: string;
+}
+
+interface Props {
+  article: InsightArticle | null;
+  /** OG share URL params for unfurl */
+  ogParams?: { metric?: string; value?: string; trend?: string };
+}
+
+export default function FeaturedInsightPanel({ article, ogParams }: Props) {
+  const t = useTranslations("featuredInsight");
+  const [copied, setCopied] = useState(false);
+
+  if (!article) {
+    return (
+      <div className="bg-card border border-border/50 rounded-2xl p-8 text-center">
+        <p className="text-sm text-muted-foreground">{t("empty")}</p>
+      </div>
+    );
+  }
+
+  const publishedDate = new Date(article.publishedAt).toLocaleDateString("en-AE", {
+    day: "numeric",
+    month: "long",
+    year: "numeric",
+  });
+
+  // Build share URL with OG params + UTM
+  const buildShareUrl = (utmSource: string) => {
+    const base = typeof window !== "undefined" ? window.location.origin : "https://staging.binayahhub.com";
+    const params = new URLSearchParams({
+      utm_source: utmSource,
+      utm_medium: "share",
+      utm_campaign: "pulse-2026-04",
+      ...(ogParams?.metric ? { metric: ogParams.metric } : {}),
+      ...(ogParams?.value ? { value: ogParams.value } : {}),
+      ...(ogParams?.trend ? { trend: ogParams.trend } : {}),
+    });
+    return `${article.url}?${params.toString()}`;
+  };
+
+  const handleCopy = async () => {
+    try {
+      await navigator.clipboard.writeText(buildShareUrl("clipboard"));
+      setCopied(true);
+      setTimeout(() => setCopied(false), 2000);
+    } catch {
+      // fallback silently
+    }
+  };
+
+  const handleWhatsApp = () => {
+    const url = `https://wa.me/?text=${encodeURIComponent(`${article.title} — ${buildShareUrl("whatsapp")}`)}`;
+    window.open(url, "_blank", "noopener,noreferrer");
+  };
+
+  const handleX = () => {
+    const url = `https://twitter.com/intent/tweet?text=${encodeURIComponent(article.title)}&url=${encodeURIComponent(buildShareUrl("twitter"))}`;
+    window.open(url, "_blank", "noopener,noreferrer");
+  };
+
+  const excerpt = article.summary.slice(0, 180) + (article.summary.length > 180 ? "…" : "");
+
+  return (
+    <div className="bg-card border border-border/50 rounded-2xl overflow-hidden">
+      {/* 60/40 layout on desktop — image left, content right */}
+      <div className="flex flex-col lg:flex-row">
+        {/* Image — 60% on desktop */}
+        <div className="relative lg:w-[60%] aspect-[16/9] lg:aspect-auto lg:min-h-[280px] flex-shrink-0 overflow-hidden bg-muted">
+          {article.imageUrl ? (
+            <Image
+              src={article.imageUrl}
+              alt={article.title}
+              fill
+              className="object-cover transition-transform duration-600 hover:scale-[1.02]"
+              sizes="(max-width: 1024px) 100vw, 60vw"
+              unoptimized
+            />
+          ) : (
+            <div className="absolute inset-0 flex items-center justify-center bg-muted">
+              <Share2 className="h-10 w-10 text-muted-foreground/20" />
+            </div>
+          )}
+          {/* Source badge overlaid on image */}
+          <div className="absolute top-3 left-3">
+            <span
+              className="text-[10px] font-bold tracking-[0.15em] uppercase px-2 py-1 rounded-sm"
+              style={{
+                color: "hsl(43, 55%, 55%)",
+                background: "rgba(0,0,0,0.55)",
+                backdropFilter: "blur(4px)",
+              }}
+            >
+              {article.source}
+            </span>
+          </div>
+        </div>
+
+        {/* Content — 40% on desktop */}
+        <div className="flex flex-col justify-between p-5 lg:p-6 lg:w-[40%]">
+          <div>
+            {/* Eyebrow */}
+            <p
+              className="text-[10px] font-bold tracking-[0.3em] uppercase mb-2"
+              style={{ color: "hsl(43, 55%, 55%)" }}
+            >
+              {t("eyebrow")}
+            </p>
+
+            {/* Headline */}
+            <h3 className="text-base font-bold text-foreground leading-snug mb-2 line-clamp-3">
+              {article.title}
+            </h3>
+
+            {/* 2-line excerpt */}
+            <p className="text-sm text-muted-foreground leading-relaxed line-clamp-2 mb-3">
+              {excerpt}
+            </p>
+
+            {/* Date */}
+            <p className="text-[10px] text-muted-foreground/60">{publishedDate}</p>
+          </div>
+
+          {/* Share row */}
+          <div className="flex items-center gap-2 mt-4 pt-4 border-t border-border/30 flex-wrap">
+            <span className="text-[10px] font-medium text-muted-foreground mr-1">{t("shareLabel")}</span>
+
+            <button
+              onClick={handleCopy}
+              className="flex items-center gap-1 px-2.5 py-1.5 rounded-lg border border-border/50 text-[11px] font-medium text-muted-foreground hover:text-foreground hover:border-accent/40 transition-all"
+              aria-label={t("copyLink")}
+            >
+              {copied ? <CheckCheck className="h-3 w-3 text-green-500" /> : <Copy className="h-3 w-3" />}
+              {copied ? t("copied") : t("copyLink")}
+            </button>
+
+            <button
+              onClick={handleWhatsApp}
+              className="flex items-center gap-1 px-2.5 py-1.5 rounded-lg border border-border/50 text-[11px] font-medium text-muted-foreground hover:text-foreground hover:border-accent/40 transition-all"
+              aria-label={t("shareWhatsApp")}
+            >
+              <Share2 className="h-3 w-3" />
+              {t("shareWhatsApp")}
+            </button>
+
+            <button
+              onClick={handleX}
+              className="flex items-center gap-1 px-2.5 py-1.5 rounded-lg border border-border/50 text-[11px] font-medium text-muted-foreground hover:text-foreground hover:border-accent/40 transition-all"
+              aria-label={t("shareX")}
+            >
+              <Share2 className="h-3 w-3" />
+              {t("shareX")}
+            </button>
+
+            <a
+              href={buildShareUrl("direct")}
+              target="_blank"
+              rel="noopener noreferrer"
+              className="ml-auto flex items-center gap-1 text-[11px] font-semibold hover:text-accent transition-colors"
+              style={{ color: "hsl(43, 55%, 55%)" }}
+            >
+              {t("readMore")}
+              <ExternalLink className="h-3 w-3" />
+            </a>
+          </div>
+        </div>
+      </div>
+    </div>
+  );
+}
